mission.title: argo: Cross Build Images for ARM
mission.description: Cross Build Images for ARM. This issue affects 76+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=argo
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Cross Build Images for ARM

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Currently ArgoCD supports building arm64 images but doesn't publish them due to issues with cross-building with normal docker and GitHub Actions (AFAIK). There's some recent advancements that would en

steps.title: Apply the fix for Cross Build Images for ARM
steps.description:
## Summary

I added a function to publish arm64 docker image in release workflow using `docker/build-push-action`.

I tested this workflow with my repo and docker hub.
https://github.com/dragoneena12/argo-cd/actions/runs/1045272993
https://hub.docker.com/repository/docker/dragoneena12/argocd

This s
```yaml
docker run --rm -it -v $(pwd)/ui/package.json:/src/package.json:ro -v $(pwd)/ui/yarn.lock:/src/yarn.lock:ro -v $(pwd)/cache:/cache --workdir /src node:12.18.4 yarn install --cache-folder /cache
```

steps.title: Confirm Cross Build Images for ARM is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: ## Summary

I added a function to publish arm64 docker image in release workflow using `docker/build-push-action`.

I tested this workflow with my repo and docker hub.
https://github.com/dragoneena12/argo-cd/actions/runs/1045272993
https://hub.docker.com/repository/docker/dragoneena12/argocd

This should be useful for argocd users who use arm64 k8s clusters like raspi4 or M1Mac.

Checklist:

* [x]. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.
codeSnippets: The Docker context becomes huge, so not really ideal, but it does do the trick. Now building via `docker buildx` seems to work fine:

This produces images; I have not tried them yet, that is next on the agenda :D

For anyone also trying this, also a few observations worth sharing:
- Building for `arm/v7` fails for me on `go get` with TLS errors for any Google domain (works fine for github.com if using `GOPROXY=direct`). The reason seems to be that emulation is so slow, `go get` gives up before the TCP connection is established. It also takes ~60 CPU minutes to get to that point (where `arm64` only takes a few CPU seconds to get there). This can fully be a problem of my Qemu version etc (using Ubuntu 20.04 via WSL2).
- The `yarn` steps take a while on `arm64`. This seems to be because it needs to compile a lot of these dependencies, as many don't seem to have an `arm64` precompiled variant.
- webpack on native machin

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
